'''Level 85''' is the 86th level of the Backrooms. [[File:Level-85.png|link=|thumb|fig 1.0 The first known image of '''Level 85''' taken during its initial discovery.]] ==Description==
'''Level 85''' is an endless, outdoor expanse of varying appearances unique to each of its entrants. The structures on the level are constructed in a Western architectural design originating from the 1960s, indicated by the evident American culture that predominantly influences the environment; these main aspects of the level remain untouched and consistent throughout, proven by the expeditions conducted in order to reach further areas of the level. As per the architecture, all instances of entries to '''Level 85''' share the presence of a persistent anomaly that has been noticed, wherein every time a wanderer enters the level, the date should be calibrated at precisely Saturday, October 27th, 1962.The origin and nature of are still unknown, and various theories have been proposed to explain its occurrence. Most research and studies accept that the persistent anomaly is that it may be a rip in the fabric of time and space, causing it to become fixed on the aforementioned date. In addition to the gratifying architecture of the structures, buildings are also heavily decorated in a manner that suggests human life may have once resided. The small, intricate details in locations such as restaurants and shops, which are filled with a variety of goods and other commodities, contribute to the overall lively atmosphere due to the level's meticulous attention to even the tiniest details. Written texts are likewise inscribed organically, and the layout of '''Level 85''' is free of any strangely placed structures and other objects, apart from nuclear sirens scattered around the level. As previously stated, time in '''Level 85''' seems to be completely halted, as devices such as watches and clocks seem to paradoxically pause regardless of their previous functionality. This signifies that time itself within the level has wholly collapsed, and as a consequence of the collapse, the level's environment is entirely vacant and appears to be outdated. Conversely, the level is devoid of any natural deterioration or breakdown of buildings and objects alike, including any indications of any living creatures ever being present in '''Level 85''' due to the phenomenon. [[File:Level-85-2.png|link=|thumb|fig 2.0 Another image of '''Level 85'''.]] Despite its timeline, '''Level 85''' possesses modern technology in the form of electronic devices such as mobile phones, computers, and smart televisions. However, these machines are depleted of their internal components, sometimes also exhibiting signs of disrepair as evidenced by missing chunks and general instability of the technology. The observation that these devices cease to function correctly and all electronic machinery is depleted of its intrinsic components suggests that time and technology within '''Level 85''' are governed by solely its timeline, owing to its historical placement where such technologies had yet to emerge. This poses a hazard to any unsuspecting travelers with their sharp, weighty fragments. In stark contrast, technology created or in existence prior to 1962 is immune to this peculiar phenomenon. Any artifacts or commodities brought into '''Level 85''' from a different era will experience the same adverse effects described above. Technologically advanced items, such as electronics, will be reduced to fragmented shells lacking their crucial machinery, while non-technological items, such as accessories, will simply disintegrate into nothingness. Furthermore, any photographs taken within/of '''Level 85''' would mysteriously undergo an anomalous alteration process, regardless of the camera used to capture the said photograph. This effect has a corollary in the photograph's color; it incorporates a distinctive orange-tinted overlay on the image, a similar product to that of polaroids exposed to high temperatures for extended periods of time. This overlay also conceals external elements that affect the physical appearance of the image, such as ink, dust, scratches, and so on. Furthermore, the effect would correct any flaws, such as motion blur, focusing and exposure issues, and resolution; hence, images taken of/in '''Level 85''' are extremely detailed.

'''If the content mentioned above is suitable for you, click here to read the section.'''
=== Psychological Tormenting* ===
'''Level 85''' is known for its array of haphazardly placed nuclear sirens that can be seen extending out of or enclosed by structures, situated in the middle of roads, and even penetrating objects such as cars and bins. It is believed that the concentration of sirens within the level represents the state of '''Level 85''''s timeline during the height of the Cold War and the Cuban Missile Crisis, indicating a potential nuclear attack or an external force strike in contrast to the withdrawal of nuclear weapons, which took place in the Frontrooms instead. [[File:Level 85 Recording.mp3|right|thumb|An audio simulation of the sirens within '''Level 85''' blasting the psychologically tormenting emergency alarm. The volume of the audio is significantly scaled down.]] Once the countdown which typically lasts between 12-48 hours reaches its end, the sirens will blast a deafening, earsplitting emergency alert system alarm. This occurrence would primarily signify a death sentence for wanderers within the level, as it is intended to psychologically torment the person with a countdown in an unfamiliar language, exacerbating the fear of an impending disaster. A robotic voice announcing the event only adds to the unease of a person's perception of an impending atomic bomb. The alarm's volume seems to increase in amplitude which may cause a complete breakdown of '''Level 85''''s reality. As a result, there is only a minute, if any, pause in the blaring of these sirens; all victims almost always die as a result of an indirect circumstance caused by the blasting of the alarm, either by suicide, rupture of the ear canal, or simply the sheer loudness of the sound that results in internal bleeding within the lung or other organs.
'''''
Groovy

===Variations of Level 85's Environment=== |-| Town Square= ===Town Square===
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
[[File:Level-85-TownSquare.png|link=|center|thumb|fig 3.0 An image of a supermarket located in the Town Square.|500x500px]]
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
'''The Town Square''' serves as the standardized representation of the level, as it is the most frequently encountered of its five variations of '''Level 85''''s environments. Due to the area being largely dominated by malls, markets, and other retail establishments, '''the Town Square''' boasts an abundance of resources such as provisions, garb, and armaments. Additionally, various landmarks, many of which stem from [[the Frontrooms]], of diverse magnitudes and geometries can be found within '''the Town Square''', often serving as signposts for articles of significance, particularly exits, as they tend to be situated amidst their contiguous surroundings. The design of ''' the Town Square''' is somewhat more labyrinthine compared to the conventional appearance of '''Level 85''', which normalizes a human-like facade, albeit not to the extent of being bizarre. It comprises of repeating, grid-like thoroughfares that are virtually indistinguishable from cities within [[the Frontrooms]]; however, the trajectory within the level causes the repetition of the environment to be imperceptible, owing to the configuration of the edifices. |-| Suburbia= ===Suburbia===
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
[[File:Level-85-Suburbs.png|link=|center|thumb|fig 4.0 An image of a region within the Suburbia .|500x500px]]
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
'''The Suburbia''' is characterized by its stylization as a prototypical American middle-class neighborhood. The setting is situated within a sprawling desert, resembling the arid lands of the southwestern United States, with average summer temperatures reaching 100°F. The predominant structures in the environment are consisted of residential buildings, such as homes, motels, and small hotels, along with scattered community swimming pools, all contributing to the illusion of protection from potential threats. This illusion often tempts individuals into indulging in the comfort of these structures and becoming preoccupied with their pleasure. The architecture of these buildings is logically sound and strategically positioned, with an abundance of provisions and typical household appliances. Eateries in this area primarily offer frozen meals, canned food, and other processed food options, as opposed to the fresh, culinary offerings available at restaurants in '''the Town Square'''. Despite the lack of fresh food options, supplies in this area are abundant and well-stocked within the houses. However, it is worth noting that due to the peculiar nature of this environment, the shelf-life of these supplies is limited given the humid temperatures present in '''the Suburbia'''. |-| Downtown= ===Downtown===
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
[[File:Level-85-Urban.png|link=|center|thumb|fig 5.0 An image of the Downtown area.|500x500px]]
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
'''The Downtown''' region in '''Level 85''', simply referred to as "'''Downtown'''," encompasses a medley of urban districts with a plethora of industrial factories, diverse living quarters including hotels and apartments, various offices, headquarters, warehouses, and skyscrapers. The architectural diversity within this area is a testament to the eclectic blend of styles, ranging from the ornate Victorian to the prevalent 60s style, and not limited to a single homogeneous style. Its districts are also well-connected along each other within the area, with a robust public transportation network that facilitates easy access. Its expansive, industrial qualities enable the scarcity of edible food in '''the Downtown''' region; they are predominantly located on the windshields of the cars abundant in the area, although as previously mentioned, consuming these provisions poses a significant risk, as they have been exposed to the harsh conditions of the region, including prolonged exposure to sunlight, making it perilous to consume. This presents a daunting challenge for those seeking sustenance in the densely populated and industrialized '''Downtown''' region. |-| Amusement= ===Zone of Amusement===
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
[[File:Level-85-Amusement.png|link=|center|thumb|fig 6.0 An image of a Disneyland in the Zone of Amusement.|500x500px]]
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
The environment known as '''the Amusement''' or '''the Zone of Amusement''' is comprised of exterior recreational establishments such as theme parks, drive-in movie theaters, athletic arenas, and racetracks. Designed solely for entertainment purposes, '''the Zone of Amusement''' has limited access to basic necessities beyond food and beverages; however, caution should be exercised when consuming food in the area, as it may contain a unique form of sugar that could elicit undesirable effects such as dizziness, headaches, nausea, and fevers. The aesthetic appeal of the surroundings, with its vintage architectural designs and bright, vivid colors, makes '''the Zone of Amusement''' a sought-after location for those seeking a nostalgic, leisurely experience.These qualities of '''the Zone of Amusement''' may prove to be fatal for a few due to its attention-grabbing nature, potentially distracting wanderers from the said imminent and deadly event. This environment is the sole location where entry points to other environments can be found. This access will presumably restart the countdown of the activation of the myriad of nuclear sirens placed throughout '''Level 85''', implying that the level recognizes this as the wanderer's first entry, leading to the aforementioned outcome. |-| Seashore= ===Seashore===
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
[[File:Level-85-Seaside.png|link=|center|thumb|fig 7.0 One of the only 2 surviving images of the Seashore.|500x500px]]
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
'''The Seashore''' is a unique and scarcely-seen habitat that was only recently found decades after '''Level 85''''s initial discovery. This environment features a long, uninterrupted stretch of beach that is segmented by various areas resembling '''the Zone of Amusement''', and even occasionally containing entrances to the said environment. There may be numerous markets beyond the shore itself, but they can only be accessed through the mentioned segments due to the steep altitude difference that divides the shore from the upper part of the environment. Supplies within '''the Seashore''' are heavily scarce and difficult to discover, with the only visible stocks of food present within the markets, which are only rarely visited due to the difficult nature of entering their area. It is advised to simply exit '''the Zone of Amusement''' if it is possible, assuming one is attempting to find food. ==Additional Information== ===Gallery=== File:Level-85-Gallery-1.png|Vacancy 8.0 File:Level-85-Gallery-2.jpg|Wonderland 9.0 File:Level-85-Gallery-3.jpg|Streets 10.0 File:Level-85-Gallery-4.png|Evening 11.0
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}

File:Level-85-Gallery-5.jpg|Beach 12.0 File:Level-85-Gallery-6.jpg|Hang-out 13.0 File:Level-85-Gallery-7.jpg|Transportation 14.0 File:Level-85-Gallery-8.png|Joyful 15.0 ===Written Recordings of Level 85===
Attachment 1.0

'''''Antiquity's Soothing Charm'''''

{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
{{Underline|3A3A3A}}
【🎨🏠】
As I walked through the deserted streets of a 60s suburban neighborhood, I was taken aback where time seemed to have stood still. The houses remained tall and grand, their pastel exteriors unscathed by time's toll and shining bright in the sun's reflection. Their manicured lawns still lush and verdant, and the mower situated idly beside the summer-themed chairs. And the windows of the houses were dark and foreboding, with their panes of glass reflecting the overcast sky. The array of concrete streets was barren, devoid of the bustling energy that was possibly once permeated the atmosphere within. But the stillness was not oppressive—eerily palpable, even—rather it was a peaceful hush that seemed to envelop the entire neighborhood—a haunting, allure of preserved architecture. It evokes a sensation of interest in me, which I must continue my journey to explore; its beauty and stillness of the surroundings drawing me in, perhaps to greed for history hidden within.
[[File:Level-85-?-light.png|300x300px|right|link=]]













{| class="article-table" !

A bliss hidden from the eyes of residence,

A city of both archaism and modernity, locked away, unknown;

Its streets once filled with... nothingness?

But it remains, forever flown, confined and gone.

The buildings stand tall, their windows still bright,

The trees a lush green, an alluring sight,

Yet no one ever walks the streets, streetlights standing upright,

As if everyone has never forgotten, this hidden light.

There's mystery in the air, a hidden clue,

A reason of this city is kept out of view,

None say it's cursed, while none also say it's true,

That it holds secrets, better left untold, too.

But still I wander, to this place,

Through the veil of time, I see its grace,

The memories of the past, I could chase,

And the beauty of the city, I can trace.

Alas, it remains hidden, from the world outside,

Its secrets secure, no longer to hide,

For this dreamville, I won't deride,

But hold it dear, in my heart and mind.

|}

I subsequently reached the center of the community, coming upon a quaint park. Its rusted, metal swings and slides still sway gracefully in the wind; the noise of the swinging chains hardly creaks noisily, as per the leaves dwindling in a spiral and rustling softly. The stillness of the park was broken only by the distant sound of a bird, or at least an imitation of it, chirping to the deathly silence that surrounded me. I took a seat on a bench, surveying my surroundings, feeling a sense of unease at the eerie beauty of it all. The world may have ceased to be animate, but it was still so perfectly well-kept; its houses, the park, and even the cars all seemed to be waiting for their owners to return, but they never would, as though the former residents' memories and spirits are woven into this place's very fabric. These expanses of antiquity—the incognizance of the lifestyles of the people within resonates, etched upon the sands of time—places echoing the past. As though they are the closest thing to time travel, or perhaps, a form of it themselves. Exploring these places is akin to leafing through a photo album of a beloved grandfather; their memories are pictured through motionless surfaces, but it is enough to experience their lives solely from an amateurishly captured photograph. I envisioned the cascading specters of families playing in the park, kids riding their bikes on the streets, and neighbors gathering for barbecues in each other's backyards. The once lively and bustling community was now frozen in time, but its memories lingered on; their spirits cascaded away to the abyss of nirvana, while their boot steps, the architectural patterns and the novelties within, endures the imprisonment of time. But, alas, till the end of time, the cosmetic euphoria and solitude shall continue to permeate. It remains as eternity's canvas, painted with brushstrokes of time, capturing moments that unfold like a symphony, unchanging, timeless, and forever remembered in stillness. Everything—mother nature, the caretaker of creatures; gargantuan rocks, the place where all hides; dark enigmas, vortexes of darkness; and even reality itself—besides it would ultimately dissipate in the hands of time. While the suburbians perpetuate, despite all their finite qualities, and echoing into the infinite darkness with its subtle winds.
Attachment 1.1
Waltzing through its thoroughfares, I continue mesmerized by its saturated hues landscape. Every inch of this intricately detailed environment astounds me, from the scattered belongings within homes to toys that still litter parks, hinting at the sudden disappearance of their owners. It is an architectonic marvel for the absence of any human intervention responsible for its creation; these structures perfectly imitate human design and architectural style, standing tall as a testament to human ingenuity. The allure of this place only increases with each step I take, and I find myself constantly in awe of its beauty and mystery. This thought ponders whether my destiny will be altered or remain unchanged as its stillness, despite the bustling appearance, begins to unveil the underlying unsettlement it has always harbored―I reckon it is showing its true colors. Feeling a sense of unease growing within me, I considered the possibility of my fate shifting from its predetermined path. I took notice of the sirens scattered across the backyards of homes, almost like a warning signal of some kind. The haunting wail of these sirens added to the already unsettling atmosphere and further fueled my thoughts about the potential deviation of my destiny. The piercing screech of the sirens echoed in my mind, serving as a constant reminder of the unknown dangers that may lurk. Out of thin air, I could discern the voice of a soulless android counting from a hundred fading into the atmosphere. I knew, by then, that everything I've enthralled for days, explained in these words, and knew, was evanescing right before my ears. The shrill, ear-piercing wail of nuclear sirens is a sound that strikes fear into the hearts of all who hear it. It is the sound of imminent danger, the signal that warns of an impending attack or catastrophic event. Like a volcano, its once-dormant sirens erupted within seconds, showing signs of an incoming catastrophe. But instead of ash, their long, undulating tone induces a haunting atmosphere; their ashes swept across the deserted streets, filling the air with a sense of dread and hopelessness. It was a warning of what was to come, a harbinger of the impending disaster that was about to unleash its fury on the unsuspecting neighborhood. With no signs of any bolts of freedom, I witnessed the houses break apart in pieces in the distance, waiting for the same fate to approach me. Then, I realized—there was never a bomb. The sheer quantity of energy that emerged from the loudness of the sirens broke apart the entirety of the neighborhood, and even reality itself. It was a cycle full of deception all along—and I was one of the victims fooled by the trickery.

==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== * Entering a region which possesses the familiar style of the 60s within levels in the first cluster may lead one to '''Level 85'''; * Leaping through an advertisement board promoting a product originating from the 60s in [[Level 11]] would transport one to another advertisement akin to the previous one within '''Level 85''', though there is a risk of falling due to the portal-like nature of this endeavor of entry; * Riding a 60s-themed ride in [[Level 35]] will transfer one to '''Level 85''' following the end of the trip; * Noclipping anywhere within the interior of [[Level 84]] would lead one to this level; * In [[Level 123]], it is possible to be randomly sent here; * Entering a house with pristine, well-preserved qualities located in [[Level 222]] has a chance of abruptly sending one to this level; * A door which may be present in an American torpedo boat in [[Level 400]] should lead one to a random structure in '''Level 85'''; * Riding a rollercoaster with a low karma rating within [[Level 999]] may promptly lead one to '''Level 85''' amidst the ride. ===Exits=== It is extremely uncommon to exit '''Level 85''', though exits may periodically emerge in some manner, which would transfer one to a variety of levels. However, there are 3 confirmed and consistent exits, which are listed below: * Entering a large sinkhole that rarely appears will bring one to [[Level 8]]; * Swimming far enough in the ocean of the Seashore would cause an occurrence of a transition to [[Level 87]] that would lead to the level; * An exclusively accessible trapdoor that materializes amidst the psychological tormenting event within the Seashore would rarely lead one to [[Level 100]]. {{Footnotes}}
